title: Canopy and cavern growth phase
summary: Thirty-third structured packet for Eco Explorer. After lane 3 proves its first giant-tree and deeper-cave family pass, the next growth wave should extend upward into a richer canopy pocket, downward into a more layered cave continuation, and then tighten readability through tiny wayfinding and one optional crossover beat instead of opening a larger traversal rewrite.

execution_order.why_now: The first giant-tree pass should grow upward one careful step before the lane broadens further.
A stronger canopy layer makes giant-tree exploration feel more magical and complete.
The canopy must stay readable and friendly at the current screen scale.
The cave family should deepen one authored layer at a time instead of jumping to a full cave engine.
A second chamber family proves the cave lane has more than one novelty beat.
The deeper cave layer should stay cozy and navigable.

execution_order.goal: Prepare tiny vertical wayfinding and recovery cues.
execution_order.why_now: Higher and deeper spaces need a little more support before the lane grows again.

execution_order.goal: Add tiny wayfinding and recovery cues for tall and deep spaces.
execution_order.why_now: These cues should improve traversal clarity without turning into a new HUD system.

execution_order.goal: Review the wayfinding and recovery layer.
execution_order.why_now: The support layer only helps if it stays subtle.

execution_order.goal: Add one optional canopy-to-cavern crossover beat.
execution_order.why_now: A light crossover will make the expanded lane feel like a real family of spaces.
This is the gate for whether lane 3 is ready for another larger wave later.

guardrails: Do not add combat, fail-state danger, or survival framing.
Do not build a generalized cave engine or broad climbing rewrite in this phase.
Use authored canopy and cavern pockets rather than large procedural expansion.
Treat giant trees and caves as science-forward sub-ecosystems, not fantasy set pieces.
acceptance_focus: The canopy feels richer without becoming confusing.
The cave family feels deeper without becoming hostile.
Lane 3 grows into a clearer long-term pillar for vertical exploration.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-03-30-canopy-and-cavern-growth-phase.md, and this packet. Treat lane 3's next wave as a careful upward-and-downward growth pass: extend the canopy, deepen the cave family, add tiny readability supports, and connect the lane with one optional crossover beat without opening a larger traversal rewrite.
